courses.subject: MBAC
courses.title: Management: Competencies and Current Perspectives

meetings.instructor:
Dr. CISTERNAS VERA Francisco Orlando, 
Professor CHEW Seen Meng, 
Professor Wan Tay Tat WONG SUN-WAI

courses.description: The objective is to know and understand the significant events and issues that affect the business of running an organization. To discover and understand the human processes that are essential for developing leadership and management skills. The course comprises two parts: a) An experiential programme whereby students will participate in structured outdoor exercises to develop problem-solving, team building, leadership and communication skills. b) A residential course with an integrative approach to understand the complexities of leading and managing a business organization in the new millennium.
courses.learning_outcomes: Upon completing the two components of this course, students should be able to acquire knowledge in overcoming the key challenges and opportunities facing managers and leaders on a global stage.
courses.course_syllabus: Training workshops and enrichment activities include: case study, management, leadership, team building, new business venture development, community service, etc.
courses.feedback_evaluation: Mandatory Course and Teaching Evaluation (CTE) is conducted at the end of the course.
courses.required_readings: To be assigned by course teacher
courses.recommended_readings: To be assigned by course teacher

courses.subject: MBAC
courses.title: Orientation and Business Simulation
courses.description: The objective is to develop students’ leadership and management skills in meeting the challenges and opportunities in a complex and competitive business environment. The course will be delivered in the form of seminars, interactive workshops, and business simulation.
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students in MBA in Finance; or MBA in Technology and Innovation
courses.learning_outcomes: Upon completion of the course, students will be able to develop their communications and leadership skills. These competencies empower students to overcome key challenges, solve problems, and manage the corporation.
courses.course_syllabus: Concept of leadership and management through different teaching and learning activities, such as guest seminars, team building, leadership training workshops, business simulation, etc.
courses.feedback_evaluation: Mandatory Course and Teaching Evaluation (CTE) is conducted at the end of the course.
courses.required_readings: To be assigned by the instructor.
courses.recommended_readings: To be assigned by the instructor.

courses.subject: MBAC
courses.title: Business Field Study - North America
courses.description:
This course includes preparatory reading assignments, followed by a week-long residential programme in North America, and concludes with a written report, oral presentation, or other assignments.

The primary objective of conducting classroom sessions in the United States or Canada is to provide MBA students with first-hand exposure to issues related to doing business in North America, local business practices, and the influence of culture. The course may also focus on a specific business topic relevant to the location, such as marketing, entrepreneurship, or globalisation.

Learning activities include lectures delivered by faculty from top academic partner schools, seminars and discussions with local business professionals and/or government officials, and site visits to local enterprises.

(Eligible for recognition toward concentration requirements depending on course content)
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students in MBA; or JD/MBA Double Degree Programme
courses.learning_outcomes: Upon completion of the business field study, students will be able to receive first-hand exposure to some of the issues (e.g. new product management) related to doing business in North America, local business practices, and the influence of culture.
courses.course_syllabus:
(1) Economic, political and cultural environment of the visiting country/region
(2) General business environment in the visiting country/region
(3) Business opportunities and challenges of local business
(4) Local business practices
courses.feedback_evaluation: Mandatory Course and Teaching Evaluation (CTE) is conducted at the end of the course.
courses.required_readings: To be assigned by course teacher
courses.recommended_readings: To be assigned by course teacher

courses.subject: MBAC
courses.title: Selected Topics in Business
courses.description: This course provides students with an opportunity to further their knowledge in selected areas of business. Topics and issues of current and emerging interest are discussed and explored. Throughout this course, student initiative and participation in discussion and projects related to the selected topics will be emphasized.
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students in MBA; or JD/MBA Double Degree Programme; or MBA in Finance; or MBA in Technology and Innovation
courses.learning_outcomes: Learning objectives for selected topics covered under this selected topic course will depend on the topic addressed-learning objectives. For each selected topic will be determined via reference to the programme learning goals.
courses.course_syllabus: To be formulated by course teacher
courses.feedback_evaluation: Mandatory Course and Teaching Evaluation (CTE) is conducted at the end of the course.
courses.required_readings: To be assigned by course teacher
courses.recommended_readings: To be assigned by course teacher

courses.subject: MBAC
courses.title: New Venture Business Project
courses.description: This course is designed to hone students' entrepreneurial skills. To that end, each student will work in a group to develop a business plan and to devise a means for acquiring capital for a new venture. The instructors will lecture on various aspects of planning and financing start-ups, and guest speakers - entrepreneurs, venture capitalists and incubator sponsors - will describe their experience of dealing with start-ups. Students will succeed in this course only if they take the initiative to produce viable plans. The instructors are coaches and facilitators to help course participants. Students, together with the instructors, will critically assess each of the business plans.
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students in MBA; or JD/MBA Double Degree Programme
courses.learning_outcomes: After finishing this course, students are expected to have a good understanding on key issues such as how a new product/service should be created/formulated, business planning, market identification, business/revenue model, execution path and hurdles, how to write, present and pitch an eye-catching investment proposal.
courses.course_syllabus:
(1) Introduction
(2) Creativity and Innovation
(3) Value Proposition Design
(4) Business Model
(5) Opportunity Evaluation
(6) Customer Discovery
(7) Writing a Business Plan
(8) Startup Financing
(9) Entrepreneurship in Asia
(10) Startup Challenges
(11) Social Innovation
courses.feedback_evaluation: Mandatory Course and Teaching Evaluation (CTE) is conducted at the end of the course.
courses.required_readings: To be assigned by course teacher
courses.recommended_readings: To be assigned by course teacher

courses.subject: MBAC
courses.title: Group Report
courses.description: This project is designed for students who cannot join the field study in Hong Kong. It provides students with an opportunity to understand the capital market in China. Participants should submit a group research report.
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students in MBA in Finance (40087); or MBA in Finance (40324)
courses.learning_outcomes: Students will understand the operation of capital markets in China.
courses.course_syllabus: Topics should be about the development of China's financial service industry.
courses.feedback_evaluation: Mandatory Course and Teaching Evaluation (CTE) is conducted at the end of the course.
courses.required_readings: To be assigned by course teacher
courses.recommended_readings: To be assigned by course teacher

courses.subject: MBAC
courses.title: Overseas Field Study in Financial Institutions
courses.description: This course is a week-long residential programme outside Hong Kong and China. It provides students with an opportunity to be exposed to a wide spectrum of issues related to international financial operations, global business and management. Students will attend seminars and visit financial institutions or international firms. The course will be concluded with a group written report and an oral presentation. Accommodation and transportation expenses should be paid by the students.
courses.enrollment_requirement: For students in MBA in Finance (40087); or MBA in Finance (40324)
courses.learning_outcomes:
Students will:
- understand the operation of international financial markets, global business and management
- acquire awareness of, and an appreciation for the diversity and complexity of the international financial and business environment
- be able to review contemporary and relevant developments in the global or regional arena, and to explore the possible strategic alternatives to capitalize on changes in the global scenario
courses.course_syllabus: Students will attend seminars and visit financial institutions or international firms.
courses.feedback_evaluation: Mandatory Course and Teaching Evaluation (CTE) is conducted at the end of the course.
courses.required_readings: To be assigned by course teacher
courses.recommended_readings: To be assigned by course teacher
